## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.
  
  Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.
  
  Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.
  
  ## Scope
  
  The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.
  
  **Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.
  
  **Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.
  
  **Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.
  
  Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.
  
  ## Settled decisions
  
  **Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.
  
  **Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.
  
  | | Retriever | Director |
  |---|---|---|
  | Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
  | Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
  | Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |
  
  Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).
  
  **Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.
  
  **Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.
  
  **Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).
  
  **Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.
  
  **Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.
  
  **RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.
  
  ## Implementation conventions
  
  **Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)
  
  **Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.
  
  **Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.
  
  **Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.
  
  **Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.
  
  **Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
  - **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
  - **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
  - **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
  - Real-time review at each step before commit.
  - No pre-staged step enumeration.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
  - Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
  - Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
  - Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
  - Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
  - Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).
  - Image-domain muster→audit rename (separate pace covers landed code).

f3cd71c4  ₢BBAAM    Cycle 4 of BBAAM verification: complete the AUAAr base64 standardization that left decode sites broken on all platforms. Pristine-lifecycle case 3 (rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle) advanced past the BBAAS over-gate (Cycle 2) and the missing buf_fact source (Cycle 3), through 11 of 12 mantle steps successfully (depot validation, SA list, governor SA creation, IAM owner grant, keys.create with 200), then died at 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format' with 'Empty client_email in key JSON' — because the decoded key file rbgp_governor_key.json was 0 bytes despite a 3192-byte privateKeyData arriving from Google's keys.create endpoint. Root cause: openssl enc -base64 -d silently produces empty output (exit 0) when input is single-line base64 without PEM-style ~76-char line breaks. Documented OpenSSL behavior across all variants tested (https://wiki.openssl.org/index.php/Base64): 'If there is no newline on decode, it silently returns no data, which can be confusing; ... on commandline enc specify -A'. Empirically verified across three platforms / three OpenSSL variants: macOS LibreSSL 3.3.6 (system /usr/bin/openssl), macOS OpenSSL 3.5.2 (Homebrew /opt/homebrew/bin/openssl), Linux Ubuntu OpenSSL 3.0.13 (cerebro /usr/bin/openssl) — all three produce 0 bytes WITHOUT -A and full correct output WITH -A for single-line input. -A flag is documented in OpenSSL master docs, 1.1.1 docs, AND OpenBSD man pages (LibreSSL); cross-implementation stable since at least OpenSSL 1.0.0c. Why the bug went latent until now: pre-AUAAr (commit 385589f4, April 1, 2026), the code used 'base64 -d' (GNU) with 'base64 -D' (BSD) fallback — both GNU and BSD base64 silently tolerate long single-line input, so mantle worked everywhere. AUAAr's 'Standardize base64 encoding/decoding to openssl enc -base64 across 6 files' commit message captures the standardization intent but missed adding -A to decode sites. Encode sites already use -A correctly with documenting comment ('Base64url encode: -A suppresses line wrapping' at rbgo_OAuth.sh:67) — the project pattern was understood for encode, decode sites just got missed in AUAAr. Bug only triggers on actual SA key-decode against live Google API responses (single-line base64); operators rebuild SAs rarely, so dormant April 1 → today; pristine-lifecycle case 3 is the first systematic exercise of the path. User confirmed 'maybe I haven't rebuilt SAs in a while' — dormancy fits the timeline exactly. Repair (Maximal scope per operator nod): added -A to all four 'openssl enc -base64 -d' decode sites — (1) rbgp_Payor.sh:1355 (governor mantle key decode, case-3 blocking); (2) rbgg_Governor.sh:242 (sibling SA invest decode, would have blocked next cycle on invest paths); (3-4) rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:959,1187 (build provenance decodes, latent until conjure/bind/graft exercised — same root fault). Single-flag addition per site; matches project's own encode-side pattern; cross-binary verified. The fault was universal not platform-specific; all four sites were latent on every platform; Maximal-scope repair eliminates the entire bug class in one notch rather than letting other paths re-surface it. Five sites total in Tools/rbk/ have 'openssl enc -base64 -d' — the fifth (vov_veiled/FUTURE/rbv_PodmanVM.sh:228) is in FUTURE/, not active code, deliberately not touched. Verification deferred to retry-after-notch in a fresh chat (operator request: prompt for clean-chat resumption to be issued).

Cycle 1 of BBAAM verification uncovered tt/rbtd-ap.AccessProbe.payor.sh failing at zrbte_furnish_probe with 'RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER must not be empty' against blank-template RBRR (post-marshal-zero state). The Payor probe is depot-agnostic by design — it targets RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID via OAuth + CRM projects.get (rbgv_AccessProbe.sh:232-283), exercising upstream-credential health on the Payor's own GCP project; never references RBDC depot identity. But zrbte_furnish_probe (Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_cli.sh:47-73) was a single all-or-nothing furnish path used for all four probe roles (governor/director/retriever/payor), unconditionally calling zrbrr_enforce which mandates RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER per the BBAAM Step 1 enrollment in rbrr_regime.sh:50 (1-26 mandatory) and format check at rbrr_regime.sh:94-95. Coupling existed pre-BBAAM in shape but didn't bite because pre-BBAAM enrollment of the moniker was permissive; BBAAM's mandate exposed it. BBAAS (commit 4d810db7, ₢BBAAS) had already established the architectural pattern — Payor-tier operations that don't need a depot are decoupled from RBRR enforcement — at rbgp_cli.sh:56-60 for rbgp_depot_list (skip zrbrr_enforce + zrbdc_kindle). BBAAS's scope was the rbgp Payor CLI; it didn't propagate to the theurge probe path. Repair extends the same shape to zrbte_furnish_probe: read BURD_TOKEN_3 (role imprint), gate zrbrr_enforce on role != 'payor', leave zrbdc_kindle unconditional. The unconditional zrbdc_kindle differs from rbgp_cli.sh's stricter form deliberately — zrbdc_kindle does no validation, just readonly string composition (rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:73-91). Lines 73-78 derive credential file paths from RBRR_SECRETS_DIR + role names (RBDC_PAYOR_RBRO_FILE is exactly what the Payor probe's OAuth flow needs); lines 84-91 compose depot identity from RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER but produce harmless nonsense ('d-', '-gar', '-pool', 'b-') when those are empty, and the Payor probe never reads them. So leaving zrbdc_kindle unconditional both feeds the Payor probe what it needs (rbro file path) and avoids cascading kindle skips into downstream modules. Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.
  
  Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.
  
  Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.
  
  ## Scope
  
  The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.
  
  **Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.
  
  **Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.
  
  **Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.
  
  Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.
  
  ## Settled decisions
  
  **Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.
  
  **Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.
  
  | | Retriever | Director |
  |---|---|---|
  | Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
  | Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
  | Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |
  
  Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).
  
  **Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.
  
  **Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.
  
  **Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).
  
  **Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.
  
  **Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.
  
  **RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.
  
  ## Implementation conventions
  
  **Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)
  
  **Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.
  
  **Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.
  
  **Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.
  
  **Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.
  
  **Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
  - **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
  - **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
  - **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
  - Real-time review at each step before commit.
  - No pre-staged step enumeration.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
  - Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
  - Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
  - Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
  - Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
  - Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).
  - Image-domain muster→audit rename (separate pace covers landed code).

f3cd71c4  ₢BBAAM    Cycle 4 of BBAAM verification: complete the AUAAr base64 standardization that left decode sites broken on all platforms. Pristine-lifecycle case 3 (rbtdrp_governor_lifecycle) advanced past the BBAAS over-gate (Cycle 2) and the missing buf_fact source (Cycle 3), through 11 of 12 mantle steps successfully (depot validation, SA list, governor SA creation, IAM owner grant, keys.create with 200), then died at 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format' with 'Empty client_email in key JSON' — because the decoded key file rbgp_governor_key.json was 0 bytes despite a 3192-byte privateKeyData arriving from Google's keys.create endpoint. Root cause: openssl enc -base64 -d silently produces empty output (exit 0) when input is single-line base64 without PEM-style ~76-char line breaks. Documented OpenSSL behavior across all variants tested (https://wiki.openssl.org/index.php/Base64): 'If there is no newline on decode, it silently returns no data, which can be confusing; ... on commandline enc specify -A'. Empirically verified across three platforms / three OpenSSL variants: macOS LibreSSL 3.3.6 (system /usr/bin/openssl), macOS OpenSSL 3.5.2 (Homebrew /opt/homebrew/bin/openssl), Linux Ubuntu OpenSSL 3.0.13 (cerebro /usr/bin/openssl) — all three produce 0 bytes WITHOUT -A and full correct output WITH -A for single-line input. -A flag is documented in OpenSSL master docs, 1.1.1 docs, AND OpenBSD man pages (LibreSSL); cross-implementation stable since at least OpenSSL 1.0.0c. Why the bug went latent until now: pre-AUAAr (commit 385589f4, April 1, 2026), the code used 'base64 -d' (GNU) with 'base64 -D' (BSD) fallback — both GNU and BSD base64 silently tolerate long single-line input, so mantle worked everywhere. AUAAr's 'Standardize base64 encoding/decoding to openssl enc -base64 across 6 files' commit message captures the standardization intent but missed adding -A to decode sites. Encode sites already use -A correctly with documenting comment ('Base64url encode: -A suppresses line wrapping' at rbgo_OAuth.sh:67) — the project pattern was understood for encode, decode sites just got missed in AUAAr. Bug only triggers on actual SA key-decode against live Google API responses (single-line base64); operators rebuild SAs rarely, so dormant April 1 → today; pristine-lifecycle case 3 is the first systematic exercise of the path. User confirmed 'maybe I haven't rebuilt SAs in a while' — dormancy fits the timeline exactly. The fault was universal not platform-specific; all four sites were latent on every platform; Maximal-scope repair eliminates the entire bug class in one notch rather than letting other paths re-surface it. Repair extends the same shape to zrbte_furnish_probe: read BURD_TOKEN_3 (role imprint), gate zrbrr_enforce on role != 'payor', leave zrbdc_kindle unconditional. The unconditional zrbdc_kindle differs from rbgp_cli.sh's stricter form deliberately — zrbdc_kindle does no validation, just readonly string composition (rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:73-91). Lines 73-78 derive credential file paths from RBRR_SECRETS_DIR + role names (RBDC_PAYOR_RBRO_FILE is exactly what the Payor probe's OAuth flow needs); lines 84-91 compose depot identity from RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER but produce harmless nonsense ('d-', '-gar', '-pool', 'b-') when those are empty, and the Payor probe never reads them. So leaving zrbdc_kindle unconditional both feeds the Payor probe what it needs (rbro file path) and avoids cascading kindle skips into downstream modules. Verification: tt/rbtd-ap.AccessProbe.payor.sh passes 5/5 iterations with HTTP 200 against blank-template RBRR. Build green via tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh. Fast suite tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh 24/27 — 3 failures (rv_rbrr_repo / rv_rbrv_all_vessels / rv_rbrn_all_nameplates) match exactly the BBAAT/BBAAS baseline flagged in their notch commits as 'pre-existing post-marshal-zero blank-template state', none exercise probe code, not regressions from this edit. Initial first-pass also gated zrbdc_kindle on role!=payor; operator counterpoint surfaced over-gating (the user's question: 'is the issue that a kindle needs to be guarded, or should we be simply guarding _enforce?') — second pass relaxed to gate only zrbrr_enforce, which is the actual validation point. SA probes (governor/director/retriever) continue through the full enforcement path unchanged. Cycle 1 of BBAAM verification advanced one step: Payor health probe step is now reachable on blank-template RBRR; next cycle continues from this state into marshal-zero + commit and pristine-lifecycle fixture cases.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Operator scope
55	
56	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.
57	
58	## Coupling to ₣A_
59	
60	Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.
61	
62	## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)
63	
64	These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).
65	
66	**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.
67	
68	**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.
69	
70	**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:
71	
72	- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
73	- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
74	- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
75	- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`
76	
77	**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.
78	
79	**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.
80	
81	**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.
82	
83	**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).
84	
85	## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split
86	
87	JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.
88	
89	**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.
90	
91	**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM → RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).
92	
93	**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.
94	
95	**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.
96	
97	## References
98	
99	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
100	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
101	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
102	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
103	
104	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAN
105	
106	## Character
107	
108	Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.
109	
110	Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.
111	
112	Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.
113	
114	## Scope
115	
116	The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.
117	
118	**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.
119	
120	**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.
121	
122	**Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.
123	
124	Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.
125	
126	## Settled decisions
127	
128	**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.
129	
130	**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.
131	
132	| | Retriever | Director |
133	|---|---|---|
134	| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
135	| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
136	| Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |
137	
138	Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).
139	
140	**Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.
141	
142	**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.
143	
144	**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).
145	
146	**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.
147	
148	**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.
149	
150	**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.
151	
152	## Implementation conventions
153	
154	**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)
155	
156	**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.
157	
158	**Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.
159	
160	**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.
161	
162	**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.
163	
164	**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.
165	
166	## Cross-pace dependencies
167	
168	- **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
169	- **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
170	- **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
171	- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.
172	
173	## Constraints
174	
175	- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
176	- Real-time review at each step before commit.
177	- No pre-staged step enumeration.
178	
179	## Out of scope
180	
181	- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
182	- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
183	- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
184	- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
185	- Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
186	- Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).
187	- Image-domain muster→audit rename (separate pace covers landed code).

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. Runbook lives in README.md release section.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Runs concurrently with ₣A_'s remaining paces; ₣A_'s one-time cutover informs `rbw-tP`'s sequence.

## Depot identity collapse — locked design decisions (₢BBAAM)

These decisions emerged in conversation during ₢BBAAM Step 1. They persist for the rest of the pace and constrain BBAAK cases 4-5 (the follow-on pace).

**Maximal collapse.** RBRR drops `RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID`, `RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY`, `RBRR_GCB_POOL_STEM` and gains `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER` (format `^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$`, length 1–26). Project ID, GAR repo, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC kindle constants from `(RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER)`. The minimal-collapse alternative (drop only the timestamp, keep the three RBRR fields) was considered and rejected — it would leave the operator hand-pasting derivable values into RBRR after every levy.

**CLOUD_PREFIX scope.** `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows uniformly through depot project_id, GAR repo, pool stem, AND bucket (depot-affiliated resources). Payor remains on `RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX` (installation-scoped, not depot-affiliated); payor naming uniformity is out of scope for this pace.

**Naming shapes.** Type markers and pool suffixes live as literal strings inside RBDC, keeping rbdc kindle independent of rbgc kindle ordering:

- Project: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER}`
- GAR repository: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-gar` (note: `-gar` suffix, not `-repository`)
- Pool stem: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}${MONIKER}-pool`
- Bucket: `${CLOUD_PREFIX}b-${MONIKER}`

**409 posture deferred.** Timestamp drop alone creates the operator-visible "fails if exists" property: re-levying a moniker collides at project-create against the GCP soft-delete graveyard. Downstream sub-resource 409 posture is left as today — bucket fatal; AR / pools idempotent. A pre-locked redesign here would over-engineer; surface fixture-driven need before reshaping.

**Mason SA name unchanged.** `mason-${moniker}` retained; broader SA-naming uniformity deferred.

**Pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust, not payor.** Theurge-side autodetect for pristine-lifecycle cases 2-3 — family stems (e.g. `pristq`, `pristg`) and the `100000` floor — lives in `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`. Payor `rbgp_depot_list` emits per-moniker fact files; theurge consumes them and computes the next free six-digit numeric increment per family prefix. Payor stays uncoupled from fixture identity space.

**Depot fact-file shape.** Per-moniker fact files use `<moniker>.${RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT}` (extension constant resolves to `depot`). File content is one of `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_BROKEN`, `RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED`. State and extension tinder constants live in `rbgc_Constants.sh` (added in Step 1).

## Cult-verb naming decision — domain-exclusive split

JJ owns "muster" as a verb (`jjx_list`). Recipe Bottle's two unrelated muster uses both rename to avoid the collision. The two replacement verbs are **domain-exclusive** — each belongs to one domain only and does not substitute for the other.

**SA cult-verb (BBAAN surface):** muster → **roster**. Colophons `rbw-arr` (retriever), `rbw-adr` (director). Constants `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ROSTER_DIRECTOR`. Functions `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Verb-as-noun ceremony: `(Roster):`.

**Image domain (landed code, separate rename pace):** muster → **audit**. Colophons `rbw-iah/iar/iae`. Constants `RBZ_AUDIT_HALLMARKS/RELIQUARIES/ENSHRINEMENTS` and Rust `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_AUDIT_*`. Functions `rbfl_audit_hallmarks/reliquaries/enshrinements`. Spec quoin `:rbtgo_image_audit:`; spec file likely renames RBSIM → RBSIA (BBAAQ decides).

**Case discipline.** Lowercase tail letter on both new colophons preserves BBAAN's existing rule: capitals (`I`, `D`) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (`r`, `a`) for no-cloud-change observation. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) unchanged.

**Cross-pace ordering.** BBAAQ (spec sweep) lands the spec-side rename first, so spec is source-of-truth. The image-rename code pace executes after BBAAQ. BBAAN's docket has been updated to use roster vocabulary directly. BBAAC and BBAAG are touched only at colophon/verb references. BBAAR's docket is already abstract ("adapt to whatever BBAAN settles") and inherits the new vocabulary without redocketing.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAN

## Character

Foundation pace for the cult-verb refactor of the SA-management surface. Lands before any consumer touches the new surface, so the downstream paces (₢BBAAQ spec, ₢BBAAR handbook, ₢BBAAK theurge) start from a settled foundation.

Coding-only pace. Spec/README/handbook narrative surfaces are owned by siblings.

Intent-only docket. Code-level planning happens at mount time, in real-time conversation against current state.

## Scope

The SA-management interface refactors along three load-bearing axes.

**Cult-verb collapse for operational SAs (retriever, director).** Invest replaces charter and knight; divest replaces forfeit for operational SAs; **roster** replaces list (renamed from "muster" per paddock cult-verb naming decision — JJ owns "muster" as a verb). Per-role split: each cult verb has retriever and director instances. Mantle (governor) stays — singleton-per-depot is asymmetric with retriever/director and the existing verb captures it honestly.

**Producer-output discipline.** SA creation writes RBRA bytes to the assay slot exclusively. The `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` credential write retires. Assay becomes the binding producer output for credential bytes; downstream consumers (test, operator) copy from assay to role production path.

**Observation-fact convention.** Roster emits per-row facts using `<basename>.<type>` type-as-extension naming via `buf_write_fact_multi` (₢BBAAO foundation). Stdout-substring assertions retire from the consumer-facing surface.

Divest-by-role recovers email from the role's installed RBRA via REST DELETE. No gcloud (RBSK rule). Governor SA cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no standalone demantle verb.

## Settled decisions

**Cult-verb triad.** invest, divest, roster.

**Colophon scheme.** `rbw-a[rd][IDr]` — six colophons replace four (aC, aK, aL, aF). Mantle's `rbw-aM` unchanged.

| | Retriever | Director |
|---|---|---|
| Invest | `rbw-arI` | `rbw-adI` |
| Divest | `rbw-arD` | `rbw-adD` |
| Roster | `rbw-arr` | `rbw-adr` |

Letter case carries semantic load: capitals (I, D) for cloud-mutating verbs; lowercase (r) for no-cloud-change observation. Matches existing `rbw-d[lL]` precedent (lowercase `dl` for list, uppercase `dL` for levy).

**Per-role split.** Roster, invest, and divest each have retriever and director instances. No unified surface. Six functions, six tabtargets, six RBZ enrollments.

**Mantle unchanged.** `rbw-aM` and `RBZ_MANTLE_GOVERNOR` stay. Governor cleanup folds into depot-unmake; no demantle verb.

**Singleton-per-role invest.** Zero instance arg from operator. SA name derived from role + RBRR-derived value (regime-kindled, not operator-supplied).

**Producer-output.** Invest writes RBRA to assay slot exclusively. `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}` credential-write retires.

**Divest-by-role.** Zero-arg. Reads role's installed RBRA, extracts email, REST DELETE.

**RBYC / RBZ constants.** This pace emits the new vocabulary constants (`RBYC_INVEST` / `RBYC_DIVEST` / `RBYC_ROSTER`, `RBZ_*_RETRIEVER` / `RBZ_*_DIRECTOR` for each verb). ₢BBAAR consumes them downstream.

## Implementation conventions

**Function naming.** `rbgg_<verb>_<role>` shape: `rbgg_invest_retriever`, `rbgg_invest_director`, `rbgg_divest_retriever`, `rbgg_divest_director`, `rbgg_roster_retrievers`, `rbgg_roster_directors`. Matches existing `rbgg_charter_retriever` precedent. (The image-domain `rbfl_*` precedent — formerly `rbfl_muster_*`, renamed to `rbfl_audit_*` per paddock cult-verb decision — is a sibling convention in a separate domain.)

**Fact-extension constants.** All fact-extension tinder constants live in RBCC as one contiguous block. Migrate the existing `RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT` from RBGC into this block; new retriever and director fact-extension constants join it. Exact constant names follow RBCC's tinder convention at mount.

**Roster fact content.** Empty (presence-as-the-fact, matches `BUF_EXT_ALIAS` precedent). Promote later if a consumer surfaces a need.

**Divest post-delete cleanup.** After successful cloud DELETE, divest removes the role's production RBRA file. Mirrors cloud state; prevents stale-credential reuse.

**Invest preexistence posture.** Fatal if the SA already exists in cloud. Operator divests first to re-key. Matches BBAAM's "fails if exists" levy posture.

**Unmake governor-absent posture.** Depot-unmake's folded governor cleanup tolerates 404 via the existing `rbgu_http_require_ok "..." "..." 404 "not found"` pattern (already used by `rbgg_forfeit_service_account`). No special detection or fail-fast — DELETE returns 200 or 404, both fine; depot teardown proceeds.

## Cross-pace dependencies

- **₢BBAAO** (multi-fact foundation, landed): consumed for roster's per-row fact emission via `buf_write_fact_multi`.
- **₢BBAAQ** (spec sweep): consumes settled vocabulary; runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAR** (handbook sweep): consumes settled `RBYC_*` / `RBZ_*` constant renames and verb-as-noun ceremony pattern (`(Invest):`, `(Divest):`, `(Roster):`); runs after this pace.
- **₢BBAAK** (theurge consumer): consumes new colophons, the per-role fact-file convention, and the assay-as-binding-output flow; runs after this pace.

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract.
- Real-time review at each step before commit.
- No pre-staged step enumeration.

## Out of scope

- Spec/README quoin authoring (₢BBAAQ).
- Operator-handbook narrative sweep (₢BBAAR).
- Theurge consumer adaptation (₢BBAAK).
- Multi-instance RBRA path scheme (singleton-per-role path stands).
- Mantle ceremony or governor lifecycle change beyond fold-into-unmake.
- Phase 1+ of `rbw-tP` (BBAAC and onward).
- Image-domain muster→audit rename (separate pace covers landed code).

lastPrompt: mount BB

content.text:
Mounted ₣BB. Here's the state.

**Heat:** rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification (₣BB) — racing, 9/21 done. Constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine, the third release-tier that refuses to run unless marshal-zero was just committed.

**Recent work:** ₢BBAAM verification cycles 1–5 closed at f3cd71c4 (the openssl `-A` flag fix across four decode sites — universal latent bug that surfaced in pristine-lifecycle case 3). Cycle 5 ended with case 3 failing immediately post-mantle on `Failed to get admin token` — known structural gap (assay-slot consumer wire-up) explicitly slated to ₢BBAAK.

**Pace:** ₢BBAAN cult-verb-foundation [rough]. Foundation for the SA-management surface refactor. Lands before consumers (BBAAQ spec, BBAAR handbook, BBAAK theurge).

**Three load-bearing axes per docket:**
1. **Cult-verb collapse** — invest/divest/roster (per-role retriever+director) replace charter/knight/forfeit/list. Mantle unchanged.
2. **Producer-output discipline** — invest writes RBRA bytes to assay slot exclusively; `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/<instance>.rbra` retires.
3. **Observation-fact convention** — roster emits per-row facts via `buf_write_fact_multi` (BBAAO foundation).

**Surface size:** six colophons (`rbw-a[rd][IDr]`), six functions (`rbgg_<verb>_<role>`), six RBZ enrollments. Mantle (`rbw-aM`) plus governor-fold-into-unmake.

**Name assessment:** "cult-verb-foundation" fits — the verb triad is the load-bearing organizing axis; the other two axes (assay output, fact emission) hang off the new verbs. Keep.

**Proposed entry approach:**
- **Survey current surface** — read the four targeted CLIs/functions (aC/aK/aL/aF + rbgg functions), nameplate of RBYC/RBZ constants, and the existing assay-slot machinery — so we share ground truth before mutating.
- **First slice candidate:** RBYC/RBZ constants block (settled vocabulary, no behavioral risk) → then one full vertical slice end-to-end (retriever invest: function + colophon + tabtarget + RBZ enrollment + assay-write) to validate the shape before replicating across the other five.
- **Coding rhythm:** real-time review at each step, commit per mutation (per paddock Release-branch contract). No pre-staged step enumeration.

**Execution strategy:** Sequential opus — architectural choices likely at every step (RBCC tinder constant naming, assay-write call shape, divest email-recovery exact REST shape). Not parallelizable; per-role-vertical work depends on the shape settled in the first slice.

Want me to start with the surface survey, or do you want to set a different entry point?
Starting the survey. Let me first locate the key files.

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.

12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle GCP Governor - Project Orchestration
20	
21	
22	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
23	# Operational Invariants (RBGG is single writer; 409 is fatal)
24	#
25	# - Single admin actor: All RBGG operations are executed by a single admin
26	#   identity. There are no concurrent writers in the same project.
27	# - Pristine-state expectation: RBGG init/creation flows assume the project
28	#   is pristine for the resources they manage. If a resource "already exists"
29	#   (HTTP 409), that's treated as state drift or prior manual activity.
30	# - Policy: All HTTP 409 Conflict responses are fatal (buc_die). We do not
31	#   treat 409 as idempotent success anywhere in RBGG.
32	#   If you see a 409, resolve state drift first (destroy/reset), then rerun.
33	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
34	
35	set -euo pipefail
36	
37	# Multiple inclusion detection
38	test -z "${ZRBGG_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgg multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
39	ZRBGG_SOURCED=1
40	
41	######################################################################
42	# Internal Functions (zrbgg_*)
43	
44	zrbgg_kindle() {
45	  test -z "${ZRBGG_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgg already kindled"
46	
47	  test -n "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID:-}"     || buc_die "RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID is not set"
48	  test   "${#RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" -gt 0 || buc_die "RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID is empty"
49	
50	  buc_log_args 'Ensure dependencies are kindled first'
51	  zrbgc_sentinel
52	  zrbgo_sentinel
53	  zrbgu_sentinel
54	  zrbgi_sentinel
55	
56	  readonly ZRBGG_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_"
57	  readonly ZRBGG_EMPTY_JSON="${ZRBGG_PREFIX}empty.json"
58	  printf '{}' > "${ZRBGG_EMPTY_JSON}"
59	
60	  # Infix values for HTTP operations
61	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE="create"
62	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY="verify"
63	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_KEY="key"
64	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_IAM_ENABLE="api_iam_enable"
65	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CRM_ENABLE="api_crm_enable"
66	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_ART_ENABLE="api_art_enable"
67	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_BUILD_ENABLE="api_build_enable"
68	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CB_SA_ACCOUNT_GEN="cb_account_gen"
69	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CB_PRIME="cb_prime"
70	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CONTAINERANALYSIS_ENABLE="api_containeranalysis_enable"
71	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_STORAGE_ENABLE="api_storage_enable"
72	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO="project_info"
73	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE_REPO="create_repo"
74	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY_REPO="verify_repo"
75	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE_REPO="delete_repo"
76	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_REPO_POLICY="repo_policy"
77	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_RPOLICY_SET="repo_policy_set"
78	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST="list"
79	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CHECK="api_checking"
80	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE="delete"
81	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS="list_keys"
82	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE="bucket_create"
83	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_DELETE="bucket_delete"
84	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_LIST="bucket_list"
85	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_OBJECT_DELETE="object_delete"
86	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_LIENS="list_liens"
87	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_DELETE="project_delete"
88	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE="project_state"
89	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE="project_restore"
90	
91	  readonly ZRBGG_KINDLED=1
92	}
93	
94	zrbgg_sentinel() {
95	  test "${ZRBGG_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgg not kindled - call zrbgg_kindle first"
96	}
97	
98	######################################################################
99	######################################################################
100	# Capture: list required services that are NOT enabled (blank = all enabled)
101	zrbgg_required_apis_missing_capture() {
102	  zrbgg_sentinel
103	
104	  local z_token="${1:-}"
105	  test -n "${z_token}" || { echo ""; return 1; }
106	
107	  local z_missing=""
108	  local z_api=""
109	  local z_service=""
110	  local z_infix=""
111	  local z_state=""
112	  local z_code=""
113	
114	  for z_api in                       \
115	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_CRM}"      \
116	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_GAR}"      \
117	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_IAM}"      \
118	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_BUILD}"    \
119	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_ANALYSIS}" \
120	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_STORAGE}"
121	  do
122	    z_service="${z_api##*/}"
123	    z_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CHECK}_${z_service}"
124	
125	    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_api}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}" || true
126	
127	    buc_log_args 'If we cannot even read an HTTP code file, that is a processing failure.'
128	    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_infix}") || z_code=""
129	    test -n "${z_code}" || return 1
130	
131	    if test "${z_code}" = "200"; then
132	      z_state=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".state") || z_state=""
133	      test "${z_state}" = "ENABLED" || z_missing="${z_missing} ${z_service}"
134	    else
135	      buc_log_args 'Any non-200 (403/404/5xx/etc) => treat as NOT enabled'
136	      z_missing="${z_missing} ${z_service}"
137	    fi
138	  done
139	
140	  printf '%s' "${z_missing# }"
141	}
142	
143	zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key() {
144	  zrbgg_sentinel
145	
146	  local z_account_name="$1"
147	  local z_display_name="$2"
148	  local z_description="$3"
149	  local z_instance="$4"
150	  local z_role="$5"
151	
152	  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"
153	
154	  buc_step 'Get OAuth token from admin'
155	  local z_token
156	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
157	
158	  buc_step "Create request JSON for ${z_account_name}"
159	  jq -n                                      \
160	    --arg account_id "${z_account_name}"     \
161	    --arg display_name "${z_display_name}"   \
162	    --arg description "${z_description}"     \
163	    '{
164	      accountId: $account_id,
165	      serviceAccount: {
166	        displayName: $display_name,
167	        description: $description
168	      }
169	    }' > "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json" || buc_die "Failed to create request JSON"
170	
171	  buc_step 'Create service account via REST API'
172	  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}" "${z_token}" \
173	    "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json"
174	  rbgu_http_require_ok "Create service account" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}"
175	
176	  local z_sa_uid
177	  z_sa_uid=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" '.uniqueId') \
178	    || buc_die "Failed to get uniqueId from SA creation response"
179	  buc_info "Service account created: ${z_account_email} (uid: ${z_sa_uid})"
180	
181	  rbgu_poll_until_ok "SA propagation (by email)" \
182	    "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY}"
183	
184	  buc_step 'Preflight: ensure no existing USER_MANAGED keys (manual cleanup path)'
185	
186	  buc_log_args 'List keys'
187	  rbgu_http_json "GET"                                                        \
188	    "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}${RBGC_PATH_KEYS}"        \
189	                                      "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS}"
190	  rbgu_http_require_ok "List service account keys" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS}"
191	
192	  buc_log_args 'Count existing user-managed keys'
193	  local z_user_keys
194	  z_user_keys=$(jq -r '[.keys[]? | select(.keyType=="USER_MANAGED")] | length' \
195	                 "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}") \
196	    || buc_die "Failed to parse service account keys"
197	
198	  if test "${z_user_keys}" -gt 0; then
199	    buc_log_args 'Provide a console URL to delete keys manually, then rerun this command'
200	    local z_sa_email_enc="${z_account_email//@/%40}"
201	    local z_keys_url="${RBGC_CONSOLE_URL}iam-admin/serviceaccounts/details/${z_sa_email_enc}?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
202	
203	    buc_warn "Found ${z_user_keys} existing USER_MANAGED key(s) on ${z_account_email}."
204	    buc_info "Open Console, select the **Keys** tab, delete old keys, then rerun:"
205	    buc_info "  ${z_keys_url}"
206	    buc_die  "Aborting to avoid minting additional keys."
207	  fi
208	
209	  buc_step 'Generate service account key (with propagation retry)'
210	  local z_key_req="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_key_request.json"
211	  printf '%s' '{"privateKeyType": "TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE"}' > "${z_key_req}"
212	
213	  local z_key_attempt=0
214	  local z_key_infix=""
215	  local z_key_code=""
216	  while :; do
217	    z_key_attempt=$((z_key_attempt + 1))
218	    z_key_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_KEY}-attempt${z_key_attempt}"
219	    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}${RBGC_PATH_KEYS}" \
220	                                           "${z_token}" "${z_key_infix}" "${z_key_req}"
221	
222	    z_key_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_key_infix}") || z_key_code=""
223	
224	    if test "${z_key_code}" = "200"; then
225	      break
226	    fi
227	
228	    if test "${z_key_code}" = "404" && test "${z_key_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX}"; then
229	      buc_warn "keys.create returned 404 (SA write-path propagation delay), retry ${z_key_attempt}/${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX} in ${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}s..."
230	      sleep "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}"
231	      continue
232	    fi
233	
234	    rbgu_http_require_ok "Generate service account key" "${z_key_infix}"
235	  done
236	
237	  buc_step 'Extract and decode key data'
238	  local z_key_b64
239	  z_key_b64=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${z_key_infix}" '.privateKeyData') \
240	    || buc_die "Failed to extract privateKeyData"
241	  local z_key_json="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_key_${z_instance}.json"
242	  printf '%s' "${z_key_b64}" | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > "${z_key_json}" \
243	    || buc_die "Failed to decode key data"
244	
245	  buc_step 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format'
246	  local z_rbra_file="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${z_instance}.rbra"
247	
248	  local z_client_email
249	  z_client_email=$(jq -r '.client_email' "${z_key_json}") || buc_die "Failed to extract client_email"
250	  test -n "${z_client_email}" || buc_die "Empty client_email in key JSON"
251	
252	  local z_private_key
253	  z_private_key=$(jq -r '.private_key' "${z_key_json}") || buc_die "Failed to extract private_key"
254	  test -n "${z_private_key}" || buc_die "Empty private_key in key JSON"
255	
256	  local z_project_id
257	  z_project_id=$(jq -r '.project_id' "${z_key_json}") || buc_die "Failed to extract project_id"
258	  test -n "${z_project_id}" || buc_die "Empty project_id in key JSON"
259	
260	  buc_step 'Write RBRA file' "${z_rbra_file}"
261	  {
262	    printf 'RBRA_ROLE=%s\n'                  "${z_role}"
263	    printf 'RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL="%s"\n'      "$z_client_email"
264	    printf 'RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY="'; printf '%s' "$z_private_key"; printf '"\n'
265	    printf 'RBRA_PROJECT_ID="%s"\n'        "$z_project_id"
266	    printf 'RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC=1800\n'
267	  } > "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_file}"
268	
269	  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_file}"
270	
271	  rm -f "${z_key_json}"
272	  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${z_rbra_file}"
273	
274	  # Echo uniqueId to stdout for callers (all logging goes to stderr per BCG)
275	  printf '%s' "${z_sa_uid}"
276	}

278	zrbgg_create_gcs_bucket() {
279	  zrbgg_sentinel
280	
281	  local z_token="${1}"
282	  local z_bucket_name="${2}"
283	
284	  buc_log_args 'Create bucket request JSON for '"${z_bucket_name}"
285	  local z_bucket_req="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_bucket_create_req.json"
286	  jq -n --arg name "${z_bucket_name}" --arg location "${RBGC_GAR_LOCATION}" '
287	{
288	  name: $name,
289	  location: $location,
290	  storageClass: "STANDARD",
291	  lifecycle: { rule: [ { action: { type: "Delete" }, condition: { age: 1 } } ] }
292	}' > "${z_bucket_req}" || buc_die "Failed to create bucket request JSON"
293	
294	  buc_log_args 'Send bucket creation request'
295	  local z_code
296	  local z_err
297	  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_GCS_BUCKET_CREATE}" "${z_token}" \
298	                                  "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE}" "${z_bucket_req}"
299	  z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE}") || buc_die "Bad bucket creation HTTP code"
300	  z_err=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE}" '.error.message') || z_err="HTTP ${z_code}"
301	
302	  case "${z_code}" in
303	    200|201) buc_info "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} created";                    return 0 ;;
304	    409)     buc_die  "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} already exists (pristine-state violation)" ;;
305	    *)       buc_die  "Failed to create bucket: ${z_err}"                             ;;
306	  esac
307	}

309	zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture() {
310	  zrbgg_sentinel
311	
312	  local z_token="${1}"
313	  local z_bucket_name="${2}"
314	
315	  local z_list_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}/b/${z_bucket_name}/o"
316	  local z_page_token=""
317	  local z_first=1
318	
319	  while :; do
320	    buc_log_args "Build URL with optional pageToken -> ${z_first}"
321	    local z_url="${z_list_url_base}"
322	    if test -n "${z_page_token}"; then
323	      buc_log_args 'pageToken must be URL-encoded'
324	      local z_tok_enc
325	      z_tok_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_page_token}") || return 1
326	      z_url="${z_url}?pageToken=${z_tok_enc}"
327	    fi
328	
329	    buc_log_args 'Use a unique infix per page to avoid clobbering files'
330	    local z_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_LIST}${z_first}"
331	    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}"
332	
333	    local z_code
334	    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_infix}") || return 1
335	    test "${z_code}" = "200" || return 1
336	
337	    buc_log_args 'Print names from this page (if any)'
338	    buc_log_args 'Next page?'
339	    jq -r                '.items[]?.name // empty' "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}"  || return 1
340	    z_page_token=$(jq -r '.nextPageToken // empty' "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}") || return 1
341	
342	    test -n "${z_page_token}" || break
343	    z_first=$((z_first + 1))
344	  done
345	}

363	zrbgg_empty_gcs_bucket() {
364	  zrbgg_sentinel
365	
366	  local z_token="${1}"
367	  local z_bucket_name="${2}"
368	
369	  buc_log_args 'Get list of objects to delete'
370	  local z_objects
371	  z_objects=$(zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture "${z_token}" "${z_bucket_name}") || {
372	    buc_log_args 'No objects found or bucket not accessible'
373	    return 0
374	  }
375	
376	  test -n "${z_objects}" || { buc_log_args 'Bucket is empty'; return 0; }
377	
378	  buc_log_args 'Delete each object'
379	  local z_object=""
380	  local z_delete_url=""
381	  local z_delete_code=""
382	  while IFS= read -r z_object; do
383	    test -n "${z_object}" || continue
384	    buc_log_args "Deleting object: ${z_object}"
385	
386	    local z_object_enc
387	    z_object_enc=$(rbgu_urlencode_capture "${z_object}") || z_object_enc=""
388	    test -n "${z_object_enc}" || { buc_warn "Failed to encode object name: ${z_object}"; continue; }
389	    z_delete_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}/b/${z_bucket_name}/o/${z_object_enc}"
390	
391	    rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_delete_url}" \
392	                              "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_OBJECT_DELETE}"
393	    z_delete_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_OBJECT_DELETE}") || z_delete_code=""
394	    case "${z_delete_code}" in
395	      204|404) buc_log_args "Object ${z_object}: deleted or not found"                     ;;
396	      *)       buc_warn     "Object ${z_object}: Failed to delete (HTTP ${z_delete_code})" ;;
397	    esac
398	  done <<< "${z_objects}"
399	}

403

405	  local z_bucket_name="${2}"

409

411	  local z_code
412	  local z_err
413	  local z_delete_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}/b/${z_bucket_name}"
414	  rbgu_http_json "DELETE" "${z_delete_url}" \
415	                      "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_DELETE}"
416	  z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_DELETE}") || z_code=""
417	  z_err=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_DELETE}" '.error.message') || z_err="HTTP ${z_code}"
418	  case "${z_code}" in
419	    204) buc_info "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} deleted";                           return 0 ;;
420	    404) buc_warn "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} not found (already deleted)";       return 0 ;;
421	    409) buc_warn "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} not empty or has retention policy"; return 1 ;;
422	    *)   buc_warn "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} failed delete";                     return 1 ;;
423	  esac
424	}

429	rbgg_list_service_accounts() {
430	  zrbgg_sentinel
431	
432	  buc_doc_brief "List all service accounts in the project"
433	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
434	
435	  buc_step 'Listing service accounts in project: '"${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
436	
437	  buc_log_args 'Get OAuth token from admin'
438	  local z_token
439	  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token (rc=$?)"
440	
441	  buc_log_args 'List service accounts via REST API'
442	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}"
443	  rbgu_http_require_ok "List service accounts"                     "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}"
444	
445	  local z_count
446	  z_count=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}" '.accounts | length') \
447	    || buc_die "Failed to parse response"
448	
449	  if test "${z_count}" = "0"; then
450	    buc_info "No service accounts found in project"
451	    return 0
452	  fi
453	
454	  buc_step "Found ${z_count} service account(s):"
455	
456	  local z_max_width
457	  z_max_width=$(jq -r '.accounts[].email | length' "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}" | sort -n | tail -1) \
458	    || buc_die "Failed to calculate max width"
459	
460	  jq -r --argjson width "${z_max_width}" \
461	    '.accounts[] | "  " + (.email | tostring | ((" " * ($width - length)) + .)) + " - " + (.displayName // "(no display name)")' \
462	    "${ZRBGU_PREFIX}${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST}${ZRBGU_POSTFIX_JSON}" || buc_die "Failed to format accounts"
463	
464	  buc_success "Service account listing completed"
465	}
