params.intent: Establish RBCC fact-extension tinder block as the central registry for buf_write_fact_multi extensions (lowercase tinder per BCG:131,999, common prefix RBCC_fact_ext_*, bare-noun values preserving readable filenames). Migrate the two depot extensions (RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT, RBGP_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT) from RBGC kindle constants to RBCC tinder constants; seed RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever and RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director for the upcoming SA-roster cult-verb surface. Updates three rbgp_Payor.sh call sites (zrbgp_depot_state_emit emit pair, rbgp_depot_unmake fact-walk loop) plus a stale Rust comment reference in rbtdrp_pristine.rs:79. Roster fact-file shape settled at mount: basename = identity (operator-typed name, extracted from API-returned local-part by stripping ${role}- prefix); content = full email (immutable, no re-derivation needed by consumers). SA management surface decisions also crystallized in conversation: multi-instance is real (cloud-space), per-role RBRA path stays singleton (path-space orthogonal); divest is identity-keyed, regime-synthesized email (no RBRA-read coupling to the governor's box), opportunistic role-RBRA cleanup only on email match; CLOUD_PREFIX kept out of SA local-part (Mason precedent paddock-locked, decoupled from cloud-space identity). Lays foundation for next slice: RBYC verb fragments and the rbgg cult-verb function set.

attachment.prompt: Where are the names 'retriever' and 'director' defined?  Those might need to be tinder constants for consistency, use in our new fact file postfixes.

content.text:
Good catch — `retriever` and `director` are already tinder constants at `rbcc_Constants.sh:37-38`:

```bash
RBCC_role_retriever="retriever"
RBCC_role_director="director"
```

But what I just committed has them as bare literals in the fact-ext values:

```bash
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever="roster-retriever"   # ← literal "retriever"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="roster-director"     # ← literal "director"
```

That's inconsistent — the role names are duplicated as inert literals instead of being referenced from the existing tinder.

**The constraint:** tinder constants must be pure literals (BCG:131 — "no variable expansion, no computation"). To reference `${RBCC_role_retriever}` inside the value, the fact-ext constants must move from tinder (top-level) to kindle (inside `zrbcc_kindle`), where variable expansion is allowed.

**Proposed revision (new commit on top of f8fced80):**
- Remove the four tinder lines I just added.
- Add four kindle constants inside `zrbcc_kindle`, uppercase per BCG kindle convention:
  ```bash
  readonly RBCC_FACT_EXT_DEPOT="depot"
  readonly RBCC_FACT_EXT_DEPOT_PROJECT="depot-project"
  readonly RBCC_FACT_EXT_ROSTER_RETRIEVER="roster-${RBCC_role_retriever}"
  readonly RBCC_FACT_EXT_ROSTER_DIRECTOR="roster-${RBCC_role_director}"
  ```
- Flip the six call sites in `rbgp_Payor.sh` and one comment from lowercase `RBCC_fact_ext_*` → uppercase `RBCC_FACT_EXT_*`.
- Update the Rust comment + the RBGC comment to point at the uppercase name.

Net effect: role-name "retriever"/"director" appears in only one place (the existing tinder), referenced everywhere else. Block coherence preserved (all four in same shape).

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.

# You may obtain a copy of the License at

# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Common Constants - File paths and naming conventions

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBCC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBCC_SOURCED=1

# Source RBBC bootstrap constants (source-time, before kindle)
test -n "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURD_CONFIG_DIR not set - rbcc requires launcher environment"
source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source rbbc_constants.sh"

# Literal constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
RBCC_rbrs_file="../station-files/rbrs.env"
RBCC_rbrn_file="rbrn.env"
RBCC_rbra_file="rbra.env"
RBCC_rbro_file="rbro.env"
RBCC_role_governor="governor"
RBCC_role_retriever="retriever"
RBCC_role_director="director"
RBCC_role_payor="payor"
RBCC_role_assay="assay"
RBCC_onboarding_nameplate="tadmor"

# Fact-file extension tinder — multi-fact registry for buf_write_fact_multi.
# Producers emit "<basename>.<extension>" via filesystem-as-data-bus pattern;
# consumers walk fact files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR keyed on extension.
RBCC_fact_ext_depot="depot"
RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever="roster-retriever"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="roster-director"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbcc_*)

zrbcc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc already kindled"
  test -n "${BURC_TOOLS_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURC_TOOLS_DIR not set - rbcc requires BURC environment"

  # Kindle constants (depend on runtime state)
  readonly RBCC_KIT_DIR="${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"

  # Curl timeout bounds — all actionable curl sites use these
  readonly RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC=10
  readonly RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC=60

  # Bottle test fixtures sleep after rbob_charge to let services (Jupyter, PlantUML)
  # initialize before HTTP assertions. A readiness poll would be precise but adds
  # dynamic state and retry logic that bash handles poorly. Fixed delay is reliable
  # if generous — test suite runtime matters less than test reliability.
  # Increase if services grow heavier.
  readonly RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC=30

  readonly ZRBCC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbcc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbcc not kindled - call zrbcc_kindle first"
}

# eof

318	}

323	# External Functions («prefix»_*)
324

326	  z«prefix»_sentinel
327

329	  local z_param2="${2:-}"
330

348	**Kindle constant — anti-pattern:**
349	
350	```bash
351	# ❌ Kindle constant defined outside kindle
352	z«prefix»_setup() {
353	  Z«PREFIX»_CACHED_PATH="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/cache"   # VIOLATION: must be in kindle
354	}
355	
356	# ✅ Kindle constants defined in kindle, used elsewhere
357	z«prefix»_kindle() {
358	  Z«PREFIX»_CACHED_PATH="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/cache"
359	  Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1
360	}
361	```
362	
363	**Tinder vs kindle constant — choosing correctly:**
364	
365	```bash
366	# ✅ Tinder constant: pure string, no variable expansion, available at source time
367	«PREFIX»_config_file="config.env"        # lower_snake name — no kindle needed
368	
369	# ✅ Kindle constant: depends on runtime state, requires kindle
370	«PREFIX»_CONFIG_DIR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/config"  # SCREAMING name — kindle required
371	
372	# ❌ Wrong: variable expansion in tinder constant position
373	«PREFIX»_config_dir="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/config"  # VIOLATION: uses ${}, must be in kindle
374	
375	# ❌ Wrong: computation in tinder constant position
376	«PREFIX»_color_red=$(z«prefix»_color '1;31')   # VIOLATION: $() — must be in kindle
377	```
378	
379	## Variable Handling (General Rules)
380	
381	### Expansion Requirements
382	
383	**Always use braced, quoted expansion:**
384	```bash
385	"${z_var}"              # Required pattern - prevents word splitting and glob expansion
386	"${Z_MODULE_VAR}"       # Module variables from kindle
387	"${1:-}"                # Parameters with defaults
388	```

```bash
# «shebang»
# «copyright»

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module «prefix» multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
Z«PREFIX»_SOURCED=1

# Tinder constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
# «PREFIX»_some_path="fixed-filename.env"
# «PREFIX»_some_prefix="prefix_"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (z«prefix»_*)

z«prefix»_kindle() {
  test -z "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module «prefix» already kindled"

  # Validate only the BURD variables actually used
  buv_dir_exists "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}"   # If using temp files
  buv_dir_exists "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"  # If producing outputs
  test -n "${BURD_NOW_STAMP:-}" || buc_die "BURD_NOW_STAMP is unset"  # If using timestamps

  # Validate module specific environment
  buv_file_exists "${«PREFIX»_REGIME_FILE}"  # If present

  # Source credentials into environment only - never write to disk
  source "${REGIME_CRED_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source credentials"

  # Kindle constant (public — safe to read from other modules; never a secret)
  «PREFIX»_PUBLIC_SELECTED_NAME="some-specific-name"

  # Kindle constants (internal)
  Z«PREFIX»_TEMP_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_temp.txt"  # Non-secret data only
  Z«PREFIX»_RESULT_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_«step_number»_result.json"
  Z«PREFIX»_CONFIG_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_«step_number»_config_"     # rest of file name appended on use
  Z«PREFIX»_MANIFEST_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/«prefix»_«command»_«step_number»_manifest_"   # rest of file name appended on use

  # Temporary files (if using BURD_OUTPUT_DIR)
  Z«PREFIX»_OUTPUT_RESULT="${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/result.txt"

  Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1
}

z«prefix»_sentinel() {
  test "${Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module «prefix» not kindled - call z«prefix»_kindle first"
}

# Helper functions follow

######################################################################
# External Functions («prefix»_*)

«prefix»_«command»() {
  z«prefix»_sentinel

  local z_param1="${1:-}"
  local z_param2="${2:-}"

  # Documentation block
  buc_doc_brief "«one_line_description»"
  buc_doc_param "«required_arg»" "«one_line_description»"
  buc_doc_oparm "«optional_arg»" "«one_line_description»"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Implementation
  test -n "${z_param1}" || buc_die "Parameter 'param1' is required"

  buc_step "«user_visible_milestone»"
  buc_log_args "«detailed_progress_information»"
  [command] || buc_die "«failure_explanation»"

  buc_success "«success_message»"
}
```

**Kindle constant — anti-pattern:**

```bash
# ❌ Kindle constant defined outside kindle
z«prefix»_setup() {
  Z«PREFIX»_CACHED_PATH="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/cache"   # VIOLATION: must be in kindle
}

# ✅ Kindle constants defined in kindle, used elsewhere
z«prefix»_kindle() {
  Z«PREFIX»_CACHED_PATH="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/cache"
  Z«PREFIX»_KINDLED=1
}
```

**Tinder vs kindle constant — choosing correctly:**

```bash
# ✅ Tinder constant: pure string, no variable expansion, available at source time
«PREFIX»_config_file="config.env"        # lower_snake name — no kindle needed

# ✅ Kindle constant: depends on runtime state, requires kindle
«PREFIX»_CONFIG_DIR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/config"  # SCREAMING name — kindle required

# ❌ Wrong: variable expansion in tinder constant position
«PREFIX»_config_dir="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/config"  # VIOLATION: uses ${}, must be in kindle

# ❌ Wrong: computation in tinder constant position
«PREFIX»_color_red=$(z«prefix»_color '1;31')   # VIOLATION: $() — must be in kindle
```

## Variable Handling (General Rules)

### Expansion Requirements

**Always use braced, quoted expansion:**
```bash
"${z_var}"              # Required pattern - prevents word splitting and glob expansion
"${Z_MODULE_VAR}"       # Module variables from kindle
"${1:-}"                # Parameters with defaults
```

content.text:
You're right — I read BCG sloppily and proposed something it explicitly forbids.

BCG:131 and BCG:372-373 are unambiguous: tinder constants must be pure literals; variable expansion in tinder position is a violation, full stop. There is no path that references `${RBCC_role_retriever}` from another tinder constant.

That means the original commit f8fced80's shape was the BCG-correct form. The "duplication" of `retriever`/`director` as bare literals in the fact-ext values is exactly what tinder discipline mandates — consistency comes from file structure (same tinder block, same `RBCC_*_lower_snake` shape) and naming convention, not from cross-reference. My subsequent proposal to promote to kindle for variable expansion was working around BCG, not following it.

Restoring the fact-ext tinder block.

#

#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Common Constants - File paths and naming conventions

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBCC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBCC_SOURCED=1

# Source RBBC bootstrap constants (source-time, before kindle)
test -n "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURD_CONFIG_DIR not set - rbcc requires launcher environment"
source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source rbbc_constants.sh"

# Literal constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
RBCC_rbrs_file="../station-files/rbrs.env"
RBCC_rbrn_file="rbrn.env"
RBCC_rbra_file="rbra.env"
RBCC_rbro_file="rbro.env"
RBCC_role_governor="governor"
RBCC_role_retriever="retriever"
RBCC_role_director="director"
RBCC_role_payor="payor"
RBCC_role_assay="assay"
RBCC_onboarding_nameplate="tadmor"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbcc_*)

zrbcc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc already kindled"
  test -n "${BURC_TOOLS_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURC_TOOLS_DIR not set - rbcc requires BURC environment"

  # Kindle constants (depend on runtime state)
  readonly RBCC_KIT_DIR="${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"

  # Curl timeout bounds — all actionable curl sites use these
  readonly RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC=10
  readonly RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC=60

  # Bottle test fixtures sleep after rbob_charge to let services (Jupyter, PlantUML)
  # initialize before HTTP assertions. A readiness poll would be precise but adds
  # dynamic state and retry logic that bash handles poorly. Fixed delay is reliable
  # if generous — test suite runtime matters less than test reliability.
  # Increase if services grow heavier.
  readonly RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC=30

  readonly ZRBCC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbcc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbcc not kindled - call zrbcc_kindle first"
}

# eof

content.text:
Tree is clean — `rbcc_Constants.sh` matches HEAD (commit f8fced80). Nothing dangling.

Yes, let's chat. I clearly missed something about your intent on the tinder discipline — you're not satisfied with "literal duplication is what BCG mandates, accept it" as an answer, and I'd rather understand why than guess again. A few angles I'm uncertain on, please pick whichever is closest:

1. **Are you challenging BCG itself here?** — i.e., the strict no-variable-expansion rule for tinder is what's producing the duplication, and you think the rule (or my reading of it) is wrong for this case.

2. **Are you saying I should look at how *existing* tinder constants in the codebase handle similar situations** — there's a pattern (in RBCC, BUF, or elsewhere) that I should be mirroring, and I'm minting something inconsistent with it?

3. **Are you saying the fact-ext block doesn't belong in tinder at all** — kindle is the right place, but my naming/derivation proposal still got something wrong?

4. **Something else entirely** — the question "where are retriever/director defined" was pointing at a different concern than what I read into it.

Which is it?

message.content: Follow BCG.  We should have and use tinder constants for 'retriever' and 'director' that are used in your fact file names.  We must have kindle constants for these somewhere, maybe 'mason' too?  'governor'?  They can all be safe tinder constants in RBCC, right?

content.text:
Re-read your message carefully. Three things:

**1. f8fced80 itself is BCG-correct.** The values `"roster-retriever"` and `"roster-director"` are pure string literals. The substring `retriever` happening to match `${RBCC_role_retriever}`'s value is not variable expansion — bash doesn't expand it, the string `"roster-retriever"` is just bytes. So that commit follows tinder discipline. No revision to that commit needed.

**2. Existing role-name tinder coverage in RBCC:**

| Role | Defined? | Where |
|---|---|---|
| governor | ✅ tinder | `RBCC_role_governor="governor"` (rbcc_Constants.sh:36) |
| retriever | ✅ tinder | `RBCC_role_retriever="retriever"` |
| director | ✅ tinder | `RBCC_role_director="director"` |
| payor | ✅ tinder | `RBCC_role_payor="payor"` |
| assay | ✅ tinder | `RBCC_role_assay="assay"` |
| **mason** | ❌ **kindle** | `RBGC_MASON_PREFIX="mason"` (rbgc_Constants.sh:55, inside zrbgc_kindle) |

Mason is the misplaced one. Per BCG:366-377 a pure literal with no runtime dependency belongs in tinder, not kindle. The other five role names live as tinder in RBCC; Mason got historically classed differently and is currently a kindle constant in a different module.

Yes — Mason can safely become tinder in RBCC (`RBCC_role_mason="mason"`), mirroring the others. Two consumers to flip: rbgp_Payor.sh:285 (the regex filter for Mason SAs) and 309 (the prefix strip for moniker derivation).

**3. "Used in your fact file names"** — the *usage* of these tinder constants doesn't appear inside tinder values (BCG forbids that). It appears in consumer function bodies. When `rbgg_roster_retrievers` filters the API roster, it'll write `^${RBCC_role_retriever}-` instead of bare `^retriever-`. That's the upcoming function work — happens when we write rbgg.

**Proposed next move:** small commit that moves Mason from RBGC kindle to RBCC tinder (`RBCC_role_mason`), updates the two rbgp_Payor.sh consumers. Lands the role-name centralization principle before we touch the cult-verb functions, so they pick up the tinder-everywhere convention.

Sound right? Or would you rather I roll Mason in alongside the rbgg cult-verb commit instead of as a standalone slice?

lastPrompt: Good to fix mason that way... but main thing is, can't you expand RBCC_role_director in +RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director?  Frankly 'roster' also deserves a tinder constant.

content.text:
Two parts to answer.

**Part 1: BCG's strict letter vs. tinder-on-tinder.**

Mechanically, yes — bash will expand `RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="roster-${RBCC_role_director}"` at source time, producing the same result as the bare literal. The expansion never reaches runtime.

But BCG:131 says "**no variable expansion, no computation, and no runtime dependency**" — that's three separate prohibitions joined by AND. BCG:372-373 categorically marks `«PREFIX»_x="${...}/y"` as VIOLATION. And the codebase has zero precedent: every tinder block in `Tools/rbk/` and `Tools/buk/` uses bare literals only — no tinder-on-tinder expansion anywhere.

So a strict BCG read says no.

But the *rationale* BCG gives is "available immediately after sourcing — no kindle required" / "no runtime dependency". Tinder-on-tinder expansion satisfies that rationale (both sides resolve at source-time, both pure literals). The "no variable expansion" rule may be over-broad if its purpose is preventing runtime coupling, since tinder-on-tinder doesn't introduce any.

I read your message as: **BCG's spirit allows tinder-on-tinder; the letter is over-strict; we should establish the convention here.** If that's right, I proceed.

If I've still got it wrong, please say plainly — I'm not going to rationalize past BCG a third time.

**Part 2: "roster" as tinder.**

Agree — the verbs `roster`, `invest`, `divest` are all pure literals appearing in multiple positions (fact-ext values, function names, colophon descriptions). Same logic as the role names. Tinder-able.

Proposed shape, all in RBCC, with tinder-on-tinder expansion:

```bash
# Cult-verbs (used in fact-file extensions and SA-management surface)
RBCC_verb_invest="invest"
RBCC_verb_divest="divest"
RBCC_verb_roster="roster"

# Mason joins the role family (currently RBGC_MASON_PREFIX kindle)
RBCC_role_mason="mason"

# Fact-extension block — derived, not duplicated
RBCC_fact_ext_depot="depot"
RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_role_retriever}"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_role_director}"
```

Effect: every role-name and verb-name literal lives in exactly one place; all downstream uses (fact-ext values, regex filters in roster functions, colophon registry) reference the tinder constant. DRY across the board.

Confirm the BCG reading and I'll land this as the next commit (rolls in the Mason move too).

content.text:
Yes. BCG is the rule book — it should govern correctly, not by override.

Four sites to repair. Here are the precise text changes:

**Site 1 — line 131 (definition of tinder constant):**

params.intent: Repair BCG tinder discipline: replace the over-strict 'no variable expansion' prohibition with the load-bearing invariant 'no runtime dependency'. Tinder-on-tinder expansion is now permitted — a tinder value may interpolate ${«PREFIX»_lower_name} for any tinder constant defined earlier in the same module's tinder block, since that expansion resolves at source time and preserves source-time availability. The original rule conflated the desired property (no runtime coupling) with a stricter mechanism (no ${} characters anywhere), forcing literal duplication of common substrings (role names, verb names) across tinder values that had no semantic justification. Four sites updated: (1) line 131 definition paragraph reshaped to declare the no-runtime-dependency invariant explicitly and call out tinder-on-tinder as permitted composition; (2) line 280 module template comment updated; (3) lines 363-377 'choosing correctly' example block expanded with a positive tinder-on-tinder example and a new ❌ kindle-constant-expansion example to mark the boundary precisely (kindle constants are unavailable at source time so their expansion still violates tinder discipline even though they're internal to the module); the existing BURD_TEMP_DIR ❌ example reframed to clarify that BURD_TEMP_DIR's runtime nature is the violation, not the ${} syntax per se; (4) line 1618 checklist item rewritten to match. Net effect: tinder constants can factor common substrings without losing source-time availability; downstream pace BBAAN's RBCC tinder enrichment (verb tinder family, mason role move, fact-ext composition) lands in the next commit consuming this rule shift.

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Common Constants - File paths and naming conventions

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBCC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBCC_SOURCED=1

# Source RBBC bootstrap constants (source-time, before kindle)
test -n "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURD_CONFIG_DIR not set - rbcc requires launcher environment"
source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source rbbc_constants.sh"

# Literal constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
RBCC_rbrs_file="../station-files/rbrs.env"
RBCC_rbrn_file="rbrn.env"
RBCC_rbra_file="rbra.env"
RBCC_rbro_file="rbro.env"
RBCC_role_governor="governor"
RBCC_role_retriever="retriever"
RBCC_role_director="director"
RBCC_role_payor="payor"
RBCC_role_assay="assay"
RBCC_onboarding_nameplate="tadmor"

# Fact-file extension tinder — multi-fact registry for buf_write_fact_multi.
# Producers emit "<basename>.<extension>" via filesystem-as-data-bus pattern;
# consumers walk fact files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR keyed on extension.
RBCC_fact_ext_depot="depot"
RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever="roster-retriever"
RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="roster-director"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbcc_*)

zrbcc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc already kindled"
  test -n "${BURC_TOOLS_DIR:-}" || buc_die "BURC_TOOLS_DIR not set - rbcc requires BURC environment"

  # Kindle constants (depend on runtime state)
  readonly RBCC_KIT_DIR="${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"

  # Curl timeout bounds — all actionable curl sites use these
  readonly RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC=10
  readonly RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC=60

  # Bottle test fixtures sleep after rbob_charge to let services (Jupyter, PlantUML)
  # initialize before HTTP assertions. A readiness poll would be precise but adds
  # dynamic state and retry logic that bash handles poorly. Fixed delay is reliable
  # if generous — test suite runtime matters less than test reliability.
  # Increase if services grow heavier.
  readonly RBCC_BOTTLE_TEST_READINESS_DELAY_SEC=30

  readonly ZRBCC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbcc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBCC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbcc not kindled - call zrbcc_kindle first"
}

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle GCP Constants - Implementation (no printf required)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
# (Module state remains ZRBGC_* per BCG; external constants use RBGC_*)
test -z "${ZRBGC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgc_*)

zrbgc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc already kindled"

  # Global Resource Naming (Google Cloud global namespace)
  # These resources compete in globally-unique namespaces across all of GCP
  # Pattern: {prefix}-{type}-{name}-{timestamp} where timestamp is YYMMDDHHMMSS
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX="rbwg"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR="p"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT="d"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET="b"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT="+%y%m%d%H%M%S"
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN=12
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX="[0-9]{${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN}}"

  # Global resource validation patterns
  # Payor:  rbwg-p-YYMMDDHHMMSS  (timestamp survives — payor is installation-scoped, not depot-scoped)
  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"

  # Basic Configuration
  readonly RBGC_ADMIN_ROLE="rbw-admin"
  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_ROLE="rbw-payor"
  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_APP_NAME="Recipe Bottle Payor"

  # Service Account Prefixes
  readonly RBGC_MASON_PREFIX="mason"

  # Timeouts
  readonly RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC=90
  readonly RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC=3
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX=7
  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC=10

  # URL Roots & Well-known Endpoints
  readonly RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL="https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM="https://iam.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM="https://cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE="https://serviceusage.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD="https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBILLING="https://cloudbilling.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE="https://storage.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_API_ROOT_SECRETMANAGER="https://secretmanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_CONSOLE_URL="https://console.cloud.google.com/"
  readonly RBGC_SIGNUP_URL="https://cloud.google.com/free"

  # OAuth Scopes
  readonly RBGC_SCOPE_CLOUD_PLATFORM="https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform"

  # Service Usage Service Identifiers
  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_IAM="iam.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_CRM="cloudresourcemanager.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY="artifactregistry.googleapis.com"

  # Email/Domain Assembly
  readonly RBGC_SA_EMAIL_DOMAIN="iam.gserviceaccount.com"

  # API Version Paths
  readonly RBGC_IAM_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CRM_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CRM_V3="/v3"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1="/v1beta1"
  readonly RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_CLOUDBILLING_V1="/v1"
  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1="/storage/v1"
  readonly RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_UPLOAD="/upload/storage/v1"
  readonly RBGC_SECRETMANAGER_V1="/v1"

  # REST Path Fragments
  readonly RBGC_PATH_PROJECTS="/projects"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_LOCATIONS="/locations"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_REPOSITORIES="/repositories"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS="/serviceAccounts"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_KEYS="/keys"

  # REST Operation Suffixes
  readonly RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":getIamPolicy"
  readonly RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX=":setIamPolicy"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX=":enable"
  readonly RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_PATH_SERVICES="/services"

  # Operation Prefixes
  readonly RBGC_OP_PREFIX_GLOBAL="operations/"

  # Ark Artifact Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Each ark type is a plain basename sibling under <prefix>hallmarks/<hallmark>/.
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE="image"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT="about"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH="vouch"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS="diags"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST="attest"
  readonly RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH="pouch"

  # GAR Categorical Namespaces (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Top-level prefix-rooted namespaces under which hallmark/reliquary/enshrine
  # arks are stored as plain basename siblings. Consumed by rbgl_GarLayout.sh.
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS="hallmarks"
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_RELIQUARIES="reliquaries"
  readonly RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_ENSHRINES="enshrines"

  # Reliquary Tool Basenames (₢A_AAK layout)
  # Canonical tool names under <prefix>reliquaries/<date>/. Authoritative
  # manifest lives in rbgji/rbgji01-inscribe-mirror.sh.
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD="gcloud"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER="docker"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE="alpine"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT="syft"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT="binfmt"
  readonly RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO="skopeo"

  # Fact-file filenames (written to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR by producers, read by tests)
  readonly RBF_FACT_HALLMARK="rbf_fact_hallmark"
  readonly RBF_FACT_BUILD_ID="rbf_fact_build_id"
  readonly RBF_FACT_GAR_ROOT="rbf_fact_gar_root"
  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_STEM="rbf_fact_ark_stem"
  readonly RBF_FACT_ARK_YIELD="rbf_fact_ark_yield"
  readonly RBF_FACT_RELIQUARY="rbf_fact_reliquary"

  # Payor fact-file filenames (governor identifying values)
  readonly RBGP_FACT_GOVERNOR_SA_EMAIL="rbgp_fact_governor_sa_email"

  # Depot lifecycle-state vocabulary. Fact-file extensions live in RBCC.
  # rbgp_depot_list emits one fact file per known depot named
  # "<moniker>.${RBCC_fact_ext_depot}" with content equal to one of the values below.
  readonly RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_COMPLETE="COMPLETE"
  readonly RBGP_DEPOT_STATE_DELETE_REQUESTED="DELETE_REQUESTED"

  # Artifact Registry (GAR) Composition
  readonly RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX="-docker.pkg.dev"


  # Canonical Role IDs
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_READER="roles/artifactregistry.reader"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_WRITER="roles/artifactregistry.writer"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_ADMIN="roles/artifactregistry.admin"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CONTAINERANALYSIS_OCCURRENCES_VIEWER="roles/containeranalysis.occurrences.viewer"
  readonly RBGC_ROLE_CLOUDBUILD_BUILDS_EDITOR="roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor"

  # Common API Base Paths (project-independent)
  readonly RBGC_API_BASE_GCS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}"

  # Cloud Resource Manager - Liens API
  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_LIST_LIENS="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"
  readonly RBGC_API_CRM_DELETE_LIEN="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens"

  # Google Cloud Storage (GCS) APIs (project-independent)
  readonly RBGC_API_GCS_BUCKETS="${RBGC_API_BASE_GCS}/b"

  readonly RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM="linux/amd64"


  # Worker pool infrastructure (dual pools: tether + airgap)
  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_TETHER="-tether"
  readonly RBGC_POOL_SUFFIX_AIRGAP="-airgap"
  readonly RBGC_WORKER_POOL_SUFFIX="-pool"
  readonly RBGC_PATH_WORKER_POOLS="/workerPools"

  readonly ZRBGC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbgc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgc not kindled - call zrbgc_kindle first"
}

# eof

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBGD_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgd multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGD_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgd_*)

zrbgd_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGD_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgd already kindled"

  # Depot-specific Constants — derive from RBDC kindle constants.
  # RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID and RBDC_GCS_BUCKET are themselves derived from
  # (RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER) at zrbdc_kindle time.

  # Service-specific Aliases
  readonly RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID="${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
  readonly RBGD_GAR_LOCATION="${RBRR_GCP_REGION}"
  readonly RBGD_GCB_PROJECT_ID="${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
  readonly RBGD_GCB_REGION="${RBRR_GCP_REGION}"

  # Project-dependent API Paths
  readonly RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE="${RBGC_PATH_PROJECTS}/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"

  # Common API Base Paths (hoisted for reuse)
  readonly RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE="${RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE}${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1}${RBGC_PATH_PROJECTS}/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_PATH_SERVICES}"
  readonly RBGD_API_BASE_CRM_PROJECT="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}${RBGC_PATH_PROJECTS}/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
  readonly RBGD_API_BASE_IAM_PROJECT="${RBGC_API_ROOT_IAM}${RBGC_IAM_V1}${RBGC_PATH_PROJECTS}/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"

  # IAM Service Accounts
  readonly RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS="${RBGD_API_BASE_IAM_PROJECT}${RBGC_PATH_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}"
  readonly RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL="${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}.${RBGC_SA_EMAIL_DOMAIN}"

  # Depot name (the moniker) and bucket — both inputs to RBGD_MASON_EMAIL and
  # bucket-IAM operations. Moniker is the operator-set RBRR field; bucket is
  # already derived in RBDC.
  readonly RBGD_DEPOT_NAME="${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER}"
  readonly RBGD_GCS_BUCKET="${RBDC_GCS_BUCKET}"
  readonly RBGD_MASON_EMAIL="${RBGC_MASON_PREFIX}-${RBGD_DEPOT_NAME}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"

  # Cloud Resource Manager (CRM) APIs
  readonly RBGD_API_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY="${RBGD_API_BASE_CRM_PROJECT}${RBGC_CRM_GET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX}"
  readonly RBGD_API_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY="${RBGD_API_BASE_CRM_PROJECT}${RBGC_CRM_SET_IAM_POLICY_SUFFIX}"
  readonly RBGD_API_CRM_GET_PROJECT="${RBGD_API_BASE_CRM_PROJECT}"
  readonly RBGD_API_CRM_DELETE_PROJECT="${RBGD_API_BASE_CRM_PROJECT}"
  readonly RBGD_API_CRM_UNDELETE_PROJECT="${RBGD_API_BASE_CRM_PROJECT}:undelete"

  # Service Usage - API Enablement
  readonly RBGD_API_SU_ENABLE_IAM="${RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE}/${RBGC_SERVICE_IAM}${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX}"
  readonly RBGD_API_SU_ENABLE_CRM="${RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE}/${RBGC_SERVICE_CRM}${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX}"
  readonly RBGD_API_SU_ENABLE_GAR="${RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE}/${RBGC_SERVICE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX}"
  readonly RBGD_API_SU_ENABLE_BUILD="${RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE}/cloudbuild.googleapis.com${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX}"
  readonly RBGD_API_SU_ENABLE_ANALYSIS="${RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE}/containeranalysis.googleapis.com${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX}"
  readonly RBGD_API_SU_ENABLE_STORAGE="${RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE}/storage.googleapis.com${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_ENABLE_SUFFIX}"

  # Service Usage - API Verification
  readonly RBGD_API_SU_VERIFY_IAM="${RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE}/${RBGC_SERVICE_IAM}"
  readonly RBGD_API_SU_VERIFY_CRM="${RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE}/${RBGC_SERVICE_CRM}"
  readonly RBGD_API_SU_VERIFY_GAR="${RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE}/${RBGC_SERVICE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}"
  readonly RBGD_API_SU_VERIFY_BUILD="${RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE}/cloudbuild.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGD_API_SU_VERIFY_ANALYSIS="${RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE}/containeranalysis.googleapis.com"
  readonly RBGD_API_SU_VERIFY_STORAGE="${RBGD_API_BASE_SERVICEUSAGE}/storage.googleapis.com"

  # Google Cloud Storage (GCS) APIs
  readonly RBGD_API_GCS_BUCKET_CREATE="${RBGC_API_GCS_BUCKETS}?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
  readonly RBGD_API_GCS_BUCKET_OPS="${RBGC_API_GCS_BUCKETS}/${RBGD_GCS_BUCKET}"
  readonly RBGD_API_GCS_BUCKET_OBJECTS="${RBGD_API_GCS_BUCKET_OPS}/o"
  readonly RBGD_API_GCS_BUCKET_IAM="${RBGD_API_GCS_BUCKET_OPS}/iam"

  readonly ZRBGD_KINDLED=1
}

zrbgd_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBGD_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgd not kindled - call zrbgd_kindle first"
}

content.text: Tinder-on-tinder resolves correctly: `roster-retriever` and `roster-director`. Notching commit 3.

params.intent: Apply the BCG tinder-on-tinder rule to enrich RBCC and centralize role-name vocabulary. Three changes land together: (1) Add RBCC_role_mason='mason' to the existing role tinder family in RBCC, retiring RBGC_MASON_PREFIX (which was a kindle constant in zrbgc_kindle for a pure literal — BCG-miscategorized; pure literals belong in tinder). The four consumers flip to RBCC_role_mason: rbgp_Payor.sh:285 (Mason-SA regex filter in zrbgp_depot_state_emit), 309 (moniker-derivation strip in same), 901 (mason name composition in zrbgp_construct_governor_token), and rbgd_DepotConstants.sh:60 (RBGD_MASON_EMAIL composition in zrbgd_kindle). RBCC sources before RBGD in every CLI that consumes RBGD, so source-time availability is preserved. (2) Add cult-verb tinder triad RBCC_verb_invest/RBCC_verb_divest/RBCC_verb_roster, establishing the verb-name centralization that the upcoming SA-management surface (rbgg invest/divest/roster functions, six new colophons) will reference uniformly. (3) Recompose the roster fact-extension values from bare literals to tinder-on-tinder expansions: RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever now derives from ${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_role_retriever}, and the director sibling parallels. Both resolve to the same source-time strings as before ('roster-retriever', 'roster-director') but the role-name and verb-name literals now live in exactly one place each. Net effect: every role-name and verb-name string this surface will use lives in one tinder definition; downstream consumers (the upcoming rbgg cult-verb functions, BBAAQ spec sweep, BBAAR handbook narrative) reference the tinder constant rather than re-typing the literal. The depot fact-ext values (depot, depot-project) remain bare literals — they have no shared substring with another tinder so composition would be ceremony without DRY benefit. Foundation slice for BBAAN cult-verb surface continues with this central vocabulary settled.

814d6bdc jjb:1011-f8fced80:₢BBAAN:n: Repair BCG tinder discipline: replace the over-strict 'no variable expansion' prohibition with the load-bearing invariant 'no runtime dependency'. Tinder-on-tinder expansion is now permitted — a tinder value may interpolate ${«PREFIX»_lower_name} for any tinder constant defined earlier in the same module's tinder block, since that expansion resolves at source time and preserves source-time availability. The original rule conflated the desired property (no runtime coupling) with a stricter mechanism (no ${} characters anywhere), forcing literal duplication of common substrings (role names, verb names) across tinder values that had no semantic justification. Four sites updated: (1) line 131 definition paragraph reshaped to declare the no-runtime-dependency invariant explicitly and call out tinder-on-tinder as permitted composition; (2) line 280 module template comment updated; (3) lines 363-377 'choosing correctly' example block expanded with a positive tinder-on-tinder example and a new ❌ kindle-constant-expansion example to mark the boundary precisely (kindle constants are unavailable at source time so their expansion still violates tinder discipline even though they're internal to the module); the existing BURD_TEMP_DIR ❌ example reframed to clarify that BURD_TEMP_DIR's runtime nature is the violation, not the ${} syntax per se; (4) line 1618 checklist item rewritten to match. Net effect: tinder constants can factor common substrings without losing source-time availability; downstream pace BBAAN's RBCC tinder enrichment (verb tinder family, mason role move, fact-ext composition) lands in the next commit consuming this rule shift.

Roster fact-file shape settled at mount: basename = identity (operator-typed name, extracted from API-returned local-part by stripping ${role}- prefix); content = full email (immutable, no re-derivation needed by consumers). SA management surface decisions also crystallized in conversation: multi-instance is real (cloud-space), per-role RBRA path stays singleton (path-space orthogonal); divest is identity-keyed, regime-synthesized email (no RBRA-read coupling to the governor's box), opportunistic role-RBRA cleanup only on email match; CLOUD_PREFIX kept out of SA local-part (Mason precedent paddock-locked, decoupled from cloud-space identity). Lays foundation for next slice: RBYC verb fragments and the rbgg cult-verb function set.
fc815a27 jjb:1011-07730eba:₢BBAAY:n: Repair the three stale axhro_* operations prose paragraphs in RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc that the previous BBAAY notch (07730eba) deliberately surfaced as itch candidates under strict docket discipline. Operator instruction was 'just do it if it is clear now and notch' — follow-up confirmed all three were unambiguously stale by visible-code contradiction, not just retired-field references that happened to live nearby. Inspecting code revealed the staleness was deeper than the original two surfaced lines: axhro_kindle (line 167-169) described an implementation generation that predates the BUK validation framework adoption (no longer uses VAR=${VAR:-} default-only kindle pattern, no compgen prefix scan, no ZRBRR_ROLLUP serialization string — confirmed via grep across rbrr_regime.sh and full project tree). Actual zrbrr_kindle in rbrr_regime.sh:30-74 enrolls fields via buv_string_enroll / buv_ipv4_enroll / buv_gname_enroll / buv_decimal_enroll under buv_group_enroll-named groups, runs buv_scope_sentinel RBRR RBRR_ for unexpected-var detection, composes ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV array (line 69) for docker -e injection, and seals via buv_lock RBRR (line 73). Replacement prose: 'Enrolls fields via buv_*_enroll type-specific helpers under named groups. Detects unexpected RBRR_ variables via buv_scope_sentinel. Composes ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV injection array and locks the regime.' Three lines, accurate, framework-truthful. axhro_validate (line 174-177) was stale on three counts: (1) named retired RBRR fields DEPOT_PROJECT_ID and GAR_REPOSITORY in 'Checks required fields' enumeration despite BBAAM/BBAAQ retiring both; (2) cited 'buv_env_* type checkers' which is not the actual API — type checking is implicit in buv_<TYPE>_enroll registration consumed by buv_vet/buv_report; (3) had separate 'Validates machine type and timeout format constraints' line that is just a subset of the type-checker contract not warranting standalone callout. Actual entry point: rbrr_cli.sh:34 calls buv_report RBRR (which delegates to buv_vet for the type-constraint checks); rbrr_regime.sh:83-90 zrbrr_enforce adds buv_dir_exists for RBRR_VESSEL_DIR and RBRR_SECRETS_DIR plus the BBAAM-era format checks (lines 94-95 DEPOT_MONIKER regex, lines 97-98 CLOUD_PREFIX regex) and joint-length cap (lines 103-109) for the 30-char GCP project ID limit. Replacement prose: 'Validates enrolled fields via buv_report RBRR against type-specific constraints from enrollment. Validates directory existence for RBRR_VESSEL_DIR and RBRR_SECRETS_DIR. Enforces RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER and RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX format and their joint-length cap (≤30 chars for GCP project ID limit).' Three lines, structured around what zrbrr_enforce actually does. axhro_render (line 182-185) was stale on four counts: (1) cited 'rbcr_* shared module' as the render path — grep across project shows no rbcr_* function namespace in active use; actual render is buv_render in BUK (rbrr_cli.sh:43 calls buv_render RBRR with title); (2) listed 5 sections 'GCP Infrastructure, Artifact Registry, Service Account Credentials, Container Runtime Configuration, Build Configuration' that don't match any of the 6 actual buv_group_enroll calls in zrbrr_kindle (Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration, Secrets Directory, Public Docs); (3) 'Displays unexpected variables in red' is wrong placement — unexpected-var detection happens at kindle time via buv_scope_sentinel, not render time; (4) 'Validates after full render completes' is wrong — render and validate are separate passes via different cli commands (rbrr_cli.sh:30 rbrr_validate vs rbrr_cli.sh:39 rbrr_render). buv_render at buv_validation.sh:789-829 walks z_buv_*_roll arrays, applies group-level gates via zbuv_group_gate_recite, displays via bupr_ presentation registry; no validation, no in-line error coloring of unexpected vars. Replacement prose: 'Renders enrolled fields via buv_render RBRR with group structure and presentation gates. Sections: Resource Prefixing, Vessel and Local Configuration, GCP Infrastructure, Google Cloud Build Configuration, Secrets Directory, Public Docs.' Two lines, accurate, dropped the two incorrect bullets. All three axhro_* paragraphs now describe the BUK-framework-era implementation truthfully. Scope-extension boundary: this notch follows the same RBSRR file as the previous BBAAY notch (07730eba), so this is a structural continuation of the same pace, not new scope. 07730eba jjb:1011-3a4ae2a5:₢BBAAY:n: Land BBAAQ Surface 4's depot-identity-collapse retirement at RBSRR (the regime spec defining rbrr.env). Carved out of BBAAV at mount time when the scope contradiction surfaced — BBAAV's docket said 'no behavioral or structural changes to spec content beyond xref flipping', but RBSRR's references to the three retired RBRR fields are definition sites, not xrefs. RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc: deleted three axhrv_variable definition blocks for the retired fields ({rbrr_depot_project_id}, {rbrr_gar_repository}, {rbrr_gcb_pool_stem}); added one axhrgv_variable block for the new {rbrr_depot_moniker} field (placed in the Resource Prefixing group between {rbrr_cloud_prefix} and {rbrr_runtime_prefix} so the depot identity tuple lives together — followed file's group convention axhrgv over docket's literal axhrv since cloud_prefix uses axhrgv); updated group intro at lines 13-16 from 'Operator-chosen prefixes' / 'Both variables must be populated' / 'The prefix must be 2-11 characters' to 'Operator-chosen identifiers' / 'All three variables must be populated' / 'The two prefixes must be 2-11 characters' since the moniker has a different format (^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$, length 1-26) than the prefix format (^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$); updated intro prose at line 8 from 'depot project identifiers, regional settings, repository locations, and service account credential file paths' to 'depot identity tuple (RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER) (from which depot project ID, GAR repository, pool stem, and bucket fall out as RBDC constants), regional settings, and service account credential file paths' — phrasing 'fall out as RBDC constants' matches paddock vocabulary at BB. Variable description for depot_moniker grounded in RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc precedent (line 31): naming 'Stem identifier that combines with {rbrr_cloud_prefix} to form the depot identity tuple', followed by format constraint and 'The tuple derives RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY, RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM, and RBDC_GCS_BUCKET at kindle time'. RBS0-SpecTop.adoc: added :rbrr_depot_moniker: <<rbrr_depot_moniker,RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER>> mapping at line 327, between rbrr_cloud_prefix and rbrr_runtime_prefix to preserve adjacency. Confirmed retired RBRR attributes (rbrr_depot_project_id, rbrr_gar_repository, rbrr_gcb_pool_stem) already absent from RBS0 mappings — BBAAQ Surface 4 had previously scrubbed them. RBSGM-governor_mantle.adoc:27 fix-or-itch decision (docket explicit: 'Decide: fix in this pace (small, local) or itch'): fixed in this pace. The require step '«DEPOT_PROJECT_ID» matches pattern rbw-*-[0-9]{12}' references the OLD timestamped project-ID shape from before BBAAM's depot-identity-collapse — under the new ${CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${MONIKER} shape (where CLOUD_PREFIX is ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*-$ and MONIKER is ^[a-z][a-z0-9]*$, length 1-26), the rbw-*-[0-9]{12} pattern can never match. Replaced lines 26-27 with a NOTE following RBSDE-depot_levy.adoc:38 precedent ('Joint length ... is enforced upstream by zrbrr_enforce at regime kindle time, so this step performs no length validation') — replacement reads 'NOTE: Format and joint length of ${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX} + d- + ${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} are enforced upstream by zrbrr_enforce at regime kindle time, so this step performs no shape validation'. Choice rationale: deleting outright would remove documentation value; updating regex to new shape would re-create stale-by-construction risk and re-validate already-validated upstream input; NOTE preserves documentation while matching established precedent in BBAAQ Surface 4's mint vocabulary. Verifications all green: grep for rbrr_depot_project_id|rbrr_gar_repository|rbrr_gcb_pool_stem in RBSRR returns zero hits; grep for rbrr_depot_moniker in RBS0 returns line 327 with new mapping; grep for rbw-*-[0-9]{12} in RBSGM returns zero hits. AsciiDoc render check requested in docket but asciidoctor not installed locally; textual scan of new variable block shows only valid linked-term references ({rbrr_cloud_prefix}, no unresolved attributes). Out-of-scope items surfaced for itch consideration during structural review of RBSRR (NOT touched per strict docket scope discipline): line 175 axhro_validate prose still says 'Checks required fields: DEPOT_PROJECT_ID, GCP_REGION, GAR_REPOSITORY' — actual validation per rbrr_regime.sh:50/94/97 now centers on RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER, RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, joint-length cap (rbrr_regime.sh:103-109), and other operational fields; line 183 axhro_render prose enumerates section names 'GCP Infrastructure, Artifact Registry, Service Account Credentials, Container Runtime Configuration, Build Configuration' — actual render groups per rbrr_regime.sh:49/53 are 'GCP Infrastructure' and 'Google Cloud Build Configuration', layout has been reorganized. Both lines describe runtime behavior of kindle/validate/render operations rather than retired field attribute references, so neither was hit by the docket's verification grep — left for itch capture or future structural pass. Also worth noting at heat-level: RBSDE precedent for derivation prose ('${RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX}d-${RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER} (resolved by RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID)') is now established across RBSDE, RBSGM, plus the seven specs BBAAV swept; RBSRR is the source-of-truth definition site reflecting that vocabulary.
